title: Hazardous Materials and Non-Mineral Waste Control Procedure
preamble: This procedure defines requirements for managing all non-mineral hazardous materials and wastes, including dangerous goods, regulated substances, sewerage and wastewater, and contaminated sites across the owner-led project.
owns: Non-mineral waste identification and characterisation requirements
Chain of custody and off-site disposal requirements
references: RTPR-HSE-PRO-0001 – Legal Requirements (waste disposal licence conditions)
RTPR-HSE-PRO-0036 – Reactive Waste (mineral waste — addressed there)
referenced_by: RTPR-HSE-PRO-0051 – Hazardous Materials (explosives as dangerous goods)

requirements.tag: [RT – RT Environment and Nature – Materials & Waste Standard]
requirements.text: Require identification and characterisation of all waste streams, maintained in a Material and Waste Register documenting: locations; inventories (annual and cumulative); and inspection requirements for contaminated sites, mineral wastes, bulk hazardous materials, permitted facilities, non-mineral wastes, and stockpiles.

requirements.tag: [IFC – IFC PS3 §4 and EHS General Guidelines §1.5]
requirements.text: Classify wastes as hazardous or non-hazardous and apply appropriate management, storage, and disposal requirements for each classification.
Apply the waste management hierarchy: reduce; reuse; recycle; recover; treat; dispose — in that order. Document how the hierarchy has been applied for each significant waste stream.

requirements.tag: [RT – RT Environment and Nature – Materials & Waste Standard]
requirements.text: Require storage facilities to be managed to maintain stability and prevent pollution. Prohibit underground installation of new tanks and pipelines for hazardous or high-risk materials. Require fluorine-free firefighting foams (PFAS prohibition as per RT standard, with documented phase-out plan for any legacy systems).

requirements.tag: [RT – RT Environment and Nature – Materials & Waste Standard]
requirements.text: Require a documented chain of custody for all materials transferred off-site, demonstrating traceability from generation to final authorised disposition. Require due diligence verification of waste contractors and disposal/treatment facilities.
Ensure that off-site waste disposal is conducted by contractors who are properly licensed and regulated, and that the project retains documentation of the disposal outcome.

requirements.tag: [RT – RT Environment and Nature – Contaminated Sites Standard]
requirements.text: Require identification of known or potential contaminated sites (hydrocarbons, PFAS, metals) and investigations and risk assessments for environmental and human health impacts. Require remedial action plans or long-term management plans commensurate with risk. Maintain a Contaminated Sites Register.
Address contaminated site risks in the project's environmental assessment and implement remediation proportionate to identified risks and future land use.

Require that secondary containment drain connections are normally plugged or equipped with locked-closed valves. Direct discharge from secondary containment to the environment is prohibited without Company approval.
Require prevention of the use of banned substances including asbestos-containing materials, lead-based paints, and hexavalent chromium-containing products.
Require hydrocarbon and chemical spill kits at all locations where these substances are stored or used. Spill kits must be sized appropriately and replenished on a defined schedule.
Require that all spills are immediately controlled, contained, and reported to the Company, with cleanup implemented without delay and effectiveness confirmed.

project_completion_guidance: Develop the project waste stream inventory before construction commences: identify all expected waste types, estimated volumes, and classification (hazardous/non-hazardous). Insert as Appendix A.
Identify and vet waste transport contractors and disposal/treatment facilities for this project. Document due diligence findings. Define approved contractors and facilities.
Conduct a contaminated site assessment for the project area before disturbance. Insert the assessment findings or reference the completed assessment report.
Define project-specific sewerage and wastewater management arrangements: what type of treatment; where discharge occurs; what monitoring confirms compliance.

If any legacy PFAS-containing firefighting foam systems exist on this project site: document the justification for retention, the controls to prevent release, and the phase-out plan with a defined completion date.

external_sources.clause: RT Environment and Nature – Materials & Waste Standard
external_sources.summary: Requires identification and characterisation of all waste streams, storage facility management, prohibition of underground tanks for hazardous materials, fluorine-free firefighting foams, and documented chain of custody for all off-site material transfers.

external_sources.clause: RT Environment and Nature – Contaminated Sites Standard
external_sources.summary: Requires identification of known or potential contaminated sites, risk assessments for environmental and human health impacts, remedial or long-term management plans, and maintenance of a Contaminated Sites Register.
Requires classification of wastes as hazardous or non-hazardous and application of appropriate management, storage, and disposal requirements for each classification.
Requires comprehensive waste characterisation to identify potentially hazardous materials and set the basis for waste management design.

Requires application of the waste management hierarchy — reduce, reuse, recycle, recover, treat, dispose — in order, with documentation of how the hierarchy has been applied for each significant waste stream.
Requires that off-site waste disposal is conducted by properly licensed and regulated contractors, and that the project retains documentation of the disposal outcome.
Requires contaminated site risks to be addressed in the project's environmental assessment and remediation to be implemented proportionate to identified risks and future land use.
Requires secondary containment drain connections to be normally plugged or locked closed, prohibiting direct discharge to the environment without Company approval.
Requires prevention of use of banned substances including asbestos-containing materials, lead-based paints, and hexavalent chromium-containing products.
Requires appropriately sized hydrocarbon and chemical spill kits at all storage and use locations, replenished on a defined schedule.
Requires that all spills are immediately controlled, contained, and reported to the Company, with cleanup confirmed effective.
